skill.name: opencontext
skill.description: Persistent memory and context management for AI agents using OpenContext. Keep context across sessions/repos/dates, store conclusions, and provide document search workflows.
skill.summary: Guide AI agents to load, search, and store persistent OpenContext project memory.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: Static findings are mostly documentation examples, not hidden executable code. The skill still creates elevated risk because it asks agents to run shell commands, install a global CLI, write user-level configuration, store a database under a hidden home directory, and configure an embedding API key.

medium_findings.title: Agent-Guided Shell Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill is a guide, but it directs agents to install a global npm package, run OpenContext CLI commands, invoke other agents, run shell commands, start a local UI, and execute test/build commands. These examples are legitimate for OpenContext, but they require user consent and command review before execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are presented in fenced examples and do not execute by themselves. The risk remains real because the skill is intended for agents with Bash access and asks them to run these commands.

medium_findings.title: Persistent Hidden Home Directory Storage
medium_findings.description: The skill documents hidden home-directory paths for OpenContext contexts, a SQLite database, generated skills, command files, and MCP configuration. This is expected for OpenContext, but it can expose or modify long-lived user context outside the current repository.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented paths and init behavior directly reference hidden user-level storage and configuration. The context shows legitimate persistence, not stealth, so severity is medium.

medium_findings.title: Embedding API Key And External Service Configuration
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to configure an embedding API key and optional OpenAI-compatible API base URL. This can send indexed context to an external embedding provider when semantic search is enabled.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The API key and base URL configuration are explicit. The document does not show secret exfiltration, but embedding workflows can transmit document content to a configured provider.

low_findings.title: Informational Network References
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs include an optional embedding endpoint, a localhost UI address, and public OpenContext documentation links. No evidence found that the skill performs unauthorized network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation or configuration examples. They are not embedded in executable code and do not hide a data transfer.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography And Reconnaissance Matches
low_findings.description: The static weak-cryptography and reconnaissance alerts appear to be false positives from Markdown filenames, headings, search mode names, and citation workflow text. No evidence found of cryptographic APIs, hashing logic, or system reconnaissance commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found prose and documentation examples at the flagged locations. There is no code implementing weak cryptography or host reconnaissance.

dangerous_patterns.title: Command Execution Guidance Combined With Persistent User Files
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill combines Bash-capable workflows with instructions that modify user-level command, skill, MCP, and context storage locations. This combination should require clear user approval before commands run.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The combination is explicit in adjacent installation and path sections. It is not malicious, but it expands the blast radius beyond the current project.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential Configuration For Semantic Search
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs configuration of an embedding API key and endpoint before building an index. Users should confirm what context will be indexed and sent to the provider.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The API key setting and index build are clearly documented. The risk depends on the configured provider and indexed content.

content.value_statement: AI agents often lose project context between sessions and repositories. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a repeatable OpenContext workflow for loading background, searching prior decisions, and storing conclusions.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to load, search, create, and iterate on OpenContext documents.
Documents OpenContext CLI commands for folders, documents, search, manifests, links, and indexing.
Shows MCP tool names for listing folders, listing documents, searching, creating documents, and generating stable links.
Describes persistent storage paths for contexts, databases, generated commands, skills, and MCP configuration.
Provides multi-agent workflow examples using Claude, Codex, Gemini, and OpenContext.
limitations: It is a workflow guide and does not include an OpenContext implementation.
Semantic search requires separate embedding configuration and index building.
Some commands can modify user-level files outside the current repository.
The quality of results depends on the documents already stored in OpenContext.

use_cases.title: Resume Project Work
use_cases.description: Load project background, known pitfalls, and prior decisions before starting a new coding session.

use_cases.title: Share Context Across Agents
use_cases.description: Use OpenContext as a common memory layer while Claude plans, Gemini analyzes, and Codex tests changes.

use_cases.title: Search Team Knowledge
use_cases.description: Find stored design notes, API contracts, acceptance criteria, and dependency decisions during implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Load Project Context
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the OpenContext workflow to load background for this repository before making changes. Summarize relevant decisions, pitfalls, and files to read.

prompt_templates.title: Search Prior Decisions
prompt_templates.prompt: Search OpenContext for prior decisions about this feature. Return the most relevant documents, citations, and any conflicts I should review.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Context Note
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an OpenContext note for this decision. Include the problem, chosen approach, rejected alternatives, affected files, and follow-up actions.

prompt_templates.title: Run A Multi-Agent Context Loop
prompt_templates.prompt: Coordinate a workflow where context is loaded first, analysis results are searched and cited, implementation is tested, and final conclusions are stored in OpenContext.

output_examples.input: Load context for a new API task.
output_examples.output: The agent returns a short context brief with relevant documents, known API contracts, common pitfalls, and suggested files to inspect first.

output_examples.input: Store conclusions after a build fix.
output_examples.output: The agent records the issue, root cause, commands used, final decision, and links to stable OpenContext documents.
The agent lists matching notes, explains why each result matters, and identifies any stale or conflicting guidance.

best_practices: Review shell commands before running them, especially install, init, and UI commands.
Keep OpenContext notes concise, cited, and tied to specific decisions or workflows.
Confirm what documents will be indexed before enabling external embedding providers.
anti_patterns: Do not store secrets, private keys, or sensitive customer data in context notes.
Do not run generated init commands without checking which user-level files they modify.
Do not rely on stale context without checking dates, citations, and repository relevance.

faq.question: What does this skill help an agent do?
faq.answer: It guides an agent through loading, searching, creating, and updating OpenContext project memory.

faq.question: Does this skill include the OpenContext CLI?
faq.answer: No. It documents installation and usage commands for the separate OpenContext CLI.

faq.question: Can it work with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The metadata and workflow examples mention Claude, Codex, Claude Code, and other agent tools.

faq.question: Does semantic search require an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. The guide shows embedding API key and base URL configuration for vector or hybrid search.

faq.question: Where does OpenContext store data by default?
faq.answer: The guide documents a hidden home-directory context folder and SQLite database under .opencontext.

faq.question: What should users review before running commands?
faq.answer: Users should review package installation, initialization, MCP configuration, indexing, and any command that writes outside the repository.
